title: WebSocket connection manager and priority broadcaster
acceptance_criteria: types.ts defines WsMessage union: { type: 'snapshot'|'signal'|'trade_open'|'trade_close'|'system'; payload: object }

connection-manager.ts: maintains Set<WebSocket> of active connections; max 20 cap; on new connection attempt above cap — evict oldest IDLE socket (last message timestamp > 60s ago) or if none idle return WS close frame 1013 Try Again Later; 30s ping interval with pong tracking (dead socket removed if no pong within 10s)

broadcaster.ts: broadcastSnapshot(payload), broadcastSignal(payload), broadcastTrade(type: 'trade_open'|'trade_close', payload) — all are void (fire-and-forget); per-socket try/catch; CRITICAL messages (signal, trade_open, trade_close) are never dropped; SNAPSHOT messages are only sent if the socket's pending snapshot count is < 20 (oldest dropped otherwise); broadcast NEVER awaits or blocks
WS endpoint ws://localhost:3000/ws/stream registered on Fastify via @fastify/websocket
src/index.ts live snapshot interval: after await computeAndSaveSnapshot() resolves, calls broadcastSnapshot() with snapshot data — NON-BLOCKING
src/index.ts sim snapshot interval: after await computeAndSaveSnapshot() resolves, calls broadcastSnapshot() with snapshot data — NON-BLOCKING; BOTH call sites are wired (not just live)
trade-executor.ts openTrade(): calls broadcastTrade('trade_open', tradeData) fire-and-forget after INSERT
trade-manager.ts closeTrade(): calls broadcastTrade('trade_close', tradeData) fire-and-forget after UPDATE
Snapshot interval re-entrancy: broadcaster.broadcastSnapshot() does not await; the interval callback never blocks even if DB is slow
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
